tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Afghan Refugee Women's Cricket Team Plays Historic Match in Cambridge
stories.summary: Afghan journalist Zahra Joya watched girls return to the field in Cambridge, overwhelmed by this historical moment of resilience after the Taliban banned women's sports.

stories.headline: Paralyzed After Accident, Chris Perry Wins Adaptive Golf Championship and Fights for Paralympic Inclusion
stories.summary: After breaking his neck in two places in a 2024 bicycle accident, 40-year-old Chris Perry won adaptive golf championships and is now pushing to get the sport into the 2032 Paralympics.

stories.headline: Alabama Firefighter Stays to Finish Woman's Lawn After Responding to Medical Emergency
stories.summary: Lt. Colton Brown finished mowing the yard of a recovering woman so her family would have one less worry during their hardest time.

stories.headline: Photoswitch Drug Restores Light Perception in Blind Patients Within Days
stories.summary: Researchers revived damaged retina cells, giving patients their first glimpses of light in years—a breakthrough that could transform blindness treatment.

stories.headline: Cape Verde Makes World Cup History as Smallest Nation to Reach Knockout Stage on Debut
stories.summary: Cape Verde, a nation of just 525,000 people across 10 islands, made World Cup history by becoming the smallest nation to reach the knockout stage on their debut.

stories.headline: Texas Town's Blessing Box Restocked Multiple Times as Neighbors Rally for Families in Need
stories.summary: Ashley Vargas and her McGregor community built a blessing box that's been restocked multiple times, with 10-year-old Pepper Loden donating bracelets that say 'smile, be you, calm, hope.'

stories.headline: Critically Endangered Pangolin and Palm Civet Released Back Into the Wild After Rehabilitation
stories.summary: A rescued Chinese Pangolin and Asian Palm Civet returned to Nongkhyllem Wildlife Sanctuary after rehabilitation, marking a win for these critically endangered and vulnerable species.

stories.headline: Tiny Algae Microbots Deliver Cancer Drugs Directly to Tumors in 30 Minutes
stories.summary: Algae-based microbots, guided by ultrasound, delivered cancer drugs directly to bladder tumors in approximately 30 minutes in mouse studies, offering faster, more targeted treatment.

stories.headline: Philippine Tamaraw Population Rebounds 12%, Newborn Calves Spotted for First Time in Years
stories.summary: The world's rarest buffalo species thrives with new calves now visible across the protected park, giving hope for this critically endangered species.

stories.headline: One Woman's Foundation Gives Shelter and Hope to Homeless Families and Children
stories.summary: Scharon Koen founded her shelter in 2017 and now houses 30 vulnerable adults and children, driven by her own experience of homelessness and knowing the pain of not having a next meal.

stories.headline: At 40, Luka Modrić Becomes Oldest World Cup Assist-Maker, Orchestrates Croatia's Stunning Comeback
stories.summary: At 40 years old, legendary midfielder Luka Modrić orchestrated Croatia's stunning comeback, becoming the oldest World Cup assist-maker and advancing his team to the knockout stage.

stories.headline: Community Raises $47,000 to Protect Endangered Butterfly's Only New England Home
stories.summary: About 400 community members raised $47,000 to protect nearly seven acres of habitat for New Hampshire's endangered Karner blue butterfly, the state's iridescent treasure found only in New England.

stories.headline: Wet Coffee Grounds Transformed Into High-Quality Fuel in Just 90 Seconds
stories.summary: South Korean researchers turned soggy coffee waste into biochar fuel without energy-intensive drying, opening new circular economy possibilities.
